methodology.summary: CI / PR gate that blocks merges on new accessibility regressions against a baseline budget, with an explicit waiver workflow for documented exceptions.

A/B testing runtime implementation covering deterministic variant assignment, exposure and conversion event tracking, statistical analysis (z-test for proportions, Wilson CI), and Sample Ratio Mismatch detection.
End-to-end playbook for abuse case test cookbook that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.
WCAG 2.
End-to-end playbook for accessibility automation qa that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.
Twelve-check rubric for reviewing AI-generated diffs (Copilot, Claude, Cursor) before merge — hallucinated APIs, silent-skip tests, convention drift, supply-chain risk.
End-to-end playbook for ai diff size discipline that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.

A packaged set of ruff and ESLint lint presets that catch the dominant AI-code smells — unused imports, fabricated APIs, no-op tests, broad excepts, console-log left over from prototyping — so the daily PR review pass enforces them automatically.
Reusable template for ai generated test validation that codifies the structure, named fields, and decision points so each new instance ships in minutes instead of being re-invented.
Evaluation method for ai llm workload architecture that defines test set, scoring function, pass/fail thresholds, and regression rules so quality is comparable across runs.
Periodic audit for solo developers: which parts of the codebase do I no longer understand, which decisions did the model make that I rubber-stamped, and how to recover.
End-to-end playbook for ai pair coding prompt patterns that walks an operator from trigger to closed outcome with named artefacts at each step.
Decision tree that decides per task whether to defer to the AI agent, co-drive, or override it before changes land.
Ai Pre Screen Bias Guardrails: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'p6-product-dev-team/Hiring screen / take-home review' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Ai Prompt As Commit Artifact: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Merge AI-Generated Code Without Bleeding' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Ai Prompt Patterns Test Ideation: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-qa-engineer/Review and trust AI-generated tests' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Alert to Fix Incident Loop: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Sentry/Datadog alert → bugfix → ship' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Android Keystore and Signing: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Ship a Mobile Build on iOS and Android' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Authentication methodology covering API Keys, JWT, OAuth 2.
Write the OpenAPI spec before any implementation code, get it reviewed like a PR, then generate server stubs and client SDKs from it.
Api Contract Pattern Selection: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Microservice extraction safety gate' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Document every API with an OpenAPI spec that includes: overview, authentication instructions, working code examples (curl + at least one SDK), all error codes with resolution hints, and a changelog.

Standardize REST API error responses using RFC 7807 Problem Details: a JSON object with `type` (URI), `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`, `traceId`, and optional `errors` array for field-level validation failures.
Centralized API gateway for microservices: routing, JWT/mTLS auth, rate limiting per consumer, CORS, request tracing via X-Request-ID, circuit breakers, and edge caching.
GraphQL allows clients to specify exactly what data they need, reducing over-fetching and under-fetching versus REST.
Define SLOs before writing any alert rules.
Every API must expose two distinct health endpoints: /healthz (liveness — process alive, no downstream calls) and /readyz (readiness — all dependencies reachable).
Every API request MUST be logged in JSON format with a unique request_id, HTTP metadata, response status and duration.
Instrument every API using the RED method (Rate, Errors, Duration) per endpoint and USE method (Utilization, Saturation, Errors) per resource.
Machine-readable HTTP API contract using OpenAPI 3.
Server-side request throttling using sliding-window, token-bucket, or fixed-window algorithms backed by a shared store (Redis/Valkey).

Design REST APIs by mapping resources (plural nouns, lowercase, hyphenated) to HTTP methods (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE), returning correct status codes for each outcome, using query parameters for filtering and sorting, and nesting sub-resources for ownership relationships.
API tests are organized in a pyramid: many unit tests, some integration tests against TestClient/httpx, targeted contract tests (Pact) only across team or service boundaries, and few E2E tests.
A methodology for managing breaking changes in HTTP APIs.

App Store and Play Store Release: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Ship a Mobile Build on iOS and Android' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Arch Health Weekly Report Template: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Architecture fitness-function check (weekly)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Architect Mentoring Curriculum: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Junior / mid dev architecture mentoring (weekly 1:1)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

A thin, opinionated geek-tier architect index that re-anchors the 50+ geek/sdlc-ai entries (spec-kit, plan-mode, RAG, graph reviewer) from the software architect's point of view — closing the perceived coverage gap without authoring new content.
Architectural Impact Pr Ranking: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'p6-product-dev-team/Weekly architectural review (45 min)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Architecture Proposal Document Template: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Greenfield architecture proposal — concept to signed-off design pack' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Architecture Review Meeting Facilitation: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Architecture review meeting facilitation' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
An indie-hacker checklist for harvesting reusable assets — code modules, copy, UI components, illustrations — from a sunset product into a starter kit that compounds across the portfolio.

Audit Grade Api Design: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Compliance-Grade Feature Delivery (FinTech / HIPAA / PCI)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Audit Grade Code Review Checklist: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Audit-grade code review for compliance client' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Shadow-run new code paths against the legacy system in production traffic and diff observable behavior — without a full golden-master setup or test harness rewrite.
Stacks evolve faster than training data.
Rubric that scores a PR's blast radius (services × users × reversibility) and routes it to deep-read vs light-skim review, making daily multi-PR review tractable for solo devs and outsource leads.
Headless browser automation using Playwright (preferred) or Puppeteer for E2E testing, web scraping, screenshot/PDF generation, and form automation.
Bug Pattern to Lint Rule Conversion: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Pre-commit hook tune-up' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Bug Report Quality Rubric: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-qa-engineer/Ship a QA program for an AI-powered product' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Bun is a JavaScript/TypeScript runtime, bundler, test runner, and package manager in a single binary.
C4 Drift Detection From Iac: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Living architecture diagram refresh' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Multi-level caching (CDN to app in-memory to Redis/Valkey to DB) with explicit pattern selection (cache-aside, write-through, write-behind, SWR) and mandatory per-key TTL, invalidation trigger, and hit-rate monitoring.
Cap Pacelc Walkthrough: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Microservice extraction safety gate' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Capacity Bottleneck Checklist: codified engineering practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Capacity + cost-modelling exercise (pre-launch or scale-event)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Changelog Automation Conventional Commits: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Pre-merge friday cleanup (rebase + squash + changelog)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
A recipe collection for pinning legacy behavior with characterization tests before refactoring — input capture, output snapshot, edge-case sweep, and pinning the diff to a baseline.
Choreography Vs Orchestration Decision: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Microservice extraction safety gate' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Ci Prod Readiness Gates: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Make Production Readiness a PR-Level Concern' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Opinionated design pattern for CI quality gates — which checks block, which warn, which run nightly — with a budget framework that keeps PR time under control.
CLAUDE.
CLAUDE.
Clean Architecture: four concentric layers with inward-only dependencies.
Clean Architecture separates concerns into concentric layers, with dependencies pointing inward.

Client Conventions Intake: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Feature from spec to production (3 weeks, P4 client-rules edition)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Systematically extracts a client's lint rules, branching model, dependency policy, and naming conventions and packages them as guardrails for an AI coding agent so generated code stops violating local norms.

Client Handover Package: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p3-technical-freelancer/Project kickoff to handover (typical 6-12 week engagement)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Coverage reports make untested branches visible so an agent or developer can write targeted tests.
Coverage measures which lines and branches execute during tests.
Fat files (controllers >100 lines, React components >150 lines, settings >150 lines) reduce testability, increase merge conflicts, and obscure responsibility.
LLMs produce partial or incorrect diffs when a file plus its tests exceed the effective context window (~20–50k tokens).
A snapshot checklist of current quality standards for mainstream stacks (TypeScript 5/React 19/Next.

A systematic examination of source code before merging, using five comment types — BLOCKING (bugs, security, standard violations), SUGGESTION (improvements), NITPICK (style), QUESTION (unclear intent), PRAISE (good code) — and six review categories: correctness, design, maintainability, testing, performance, security.
PRs less than 400 lines; CI green before review; style delegated to formatters.

A structured approach to examining code changes before merge: reviewer reads the diff plus touched files, categorizes findings using Conventional Comments labels (blocking/suggestion/nit/question/praise), and emits at most 20 comments prioritized correctness → security → maintainability → nits.
Standardize PR descriptions, CI checks, and reviewer comment scaffolding so inconsistent review practices don't slow the cycle.

Code Review Slo And Rubric: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p6-product-dev-team/Cross-role handoff: PM -> Architect -> Dev -> QA -> DevOps in one loop' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Codemod Recipe Library: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Survive a Major Framework Upgrade Without a Big-Bang Branch' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Compliance Aware Code Review Checklist: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Compliance-Grade Feature Delivery (FinTech / HIPAA / PCI)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Compliance Control Matrix Soc2 Gdpr: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Security-by-design audit + threat-modelling cycle' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Compliance Regime Review Matrix Soc2 Hipaa Pci: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Audit-grade code review for compliance client' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Context Window Curation For Coding Agents: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p1-solo-saas-builder/AI-pair coding loop for solo SaaS (Claude/Cursor + Spec)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Continuous Delivery is a software development practice where code changes are automatically prepared for release to production.
Design API contracts (OpenAPI spec) before writing implementation code.

Contract Testing Consumer Driven: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-qa-engineer/Modernize a 2018-era QA suite into AI-augmented test ops' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Cost Modeling At Design Time: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Cost + latency budget BEFORE design freeze' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Coverage Rebuild Playbook: codified testing practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Test-coverage rebuild from 30% to 70% (6 weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS) separates read and write operations into distinct models: commands change state and return void or an ID; queries return data and never modify state.
Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS) separates read and write operations into different models.
Crash-tracking pipeline (Sentry / Crashlytics / Bugsnag) defined as code — symbol upload, sourcemap upload, alert rules, dashboards, retention — checked in and gated like any other infrastructure.
Cross Team Interface Change Log: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Cross-team architecture sync (weekly)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
ASP.
Long-running in-process background workers in ASP.
Production.
TBD — fill from v1 source
Entity Framework Core patterns for ASP.
xUnit is the de facto standard test framework for modern.

A methodology for applying CSS-in-JS in React projects: use zero-runtime libraries (`vanilla-extract`, `linaria`, `panda-css`) for RSC and SSR builds; runtime libraries (`styled-components` v6, `@emotion/styled`) only for client-only SPAs.

Data Ownership Split Patterns: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Monolith-to-services migration architecture (3 months)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Data Reconciliation Checklist: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Legacy-to-modern migration project (~3 months)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Relational schema design for integrity, performance, and maintainability.
An Aggregate is a cluster of domain objects (Entities + Value Objects) treated as a single unit for data changes.

The Anti-Corruption Layer (ACL) is an adapter in the infrastructure layer that translates between the external system's model (a legacy API, a third-party service, another Bounded Context) and your domain model.
A Domain Event records that something significant happened in the domain: OrderPlaced, SubscriptionRenewed, InvoiceIssued.
A Repository provides a collection-like interface for accessing Aggregates.
A Value Object is a domain concept defined entirely by its attributes, not by identity.
Debt Interest Vs Principal Rubric: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Weekly tech-debt prioritisation session' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Five-factor scoring rubric (User-Impact × Change-Frequency × Fragility × Blast-Radius × Fix-Cost) that produces a single numeric debt score per item, defensible to PMs and the org.
Defect Attribution By Phase: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-qa-engineer/Major release QA cycle: regression + smoke + UAT' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Dep Bump Batching Strategy delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Dependency audit + bump' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: How to split patch/minor/major bumps across PRs is solved by experience, not by faion content. Bumps are
Concrete adoption checklist for a new dependency or service edge — pin, named owner, SBOM entry, escape hatch — distinct from the trade-off decision phase.

Dependency Spike Kill Criteria Template delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Library evaluation spike' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: trade-off-* and decision-tree-* methodologies are present but high-level. A concrete spike

Deploy Notes Template With Rollback delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Deploy-to-staging + smoke + sign-off' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Continuous-delivery methodologies cover pipelines, not communication artifacts. De

Design tokens are the atomic values of a design system — colors, spacing, typography, shadows — stored as structured data with a three-tier hierarchy: primitive (raw values) → semantic (purpose-based aliases) → component (usage-specific).
Cross-stack pattern for deterministic test data — factories, frozen clocks, seeded RNG, per-test DB isolation, golden datasets, time/randomness pinning — that eliminates the bulk of suite flakiness.

Diagram As Code Mermaid Structurizr delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Architecture review meeting facilitation' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: c4-model exists but doesn't prescribe a diagram-as-code toolchain. Architects

Distributed Pitfalls Catalog delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Microservice extraction safety gate' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: 8 fallacies of distributed computing, clock skew, exactly-once myths, dual writes, distrib
Thin views plus service layer plus `drf-spectacular` OpenAPI plus `ViewSet` pattern for Django REST Framework.
Every Django domain model must inherit from BaseModel, an abstract model providing uid (UUID), created_at, and updated_at fields.

A methodology for integrating Celery into Django applications: every async task must declare explicit name, idempotency guard, retry policy (max_retries, retry_backoff, retry_jitter), and time limits (soft_time_limit, time_limit).

Structural and code conventions for Django projects establishing consistent directory layout (apps/ + core/ + config/settings/), aliased cross-app imports to prevent circular dependencies, service-layer architecture isolating business logic from HTTP concerns, keyword-only service arguments for clarity, TextChoices for model constants, and mandatory update_fields on.

A deterministic decision framework for placing code in Django applications, partitioning functionality into views (HTTP handling), services (business logic and DB writes), utils (pure functions), integrations (third-party API wrappers), tasks (async/background work), models (data definitions), and serializers (validation and formatting).
A convention for Django multi-app projects: always import cross-app modules via alias (from apps.
Django model design following the BaseModel abstract pattern (integer PK + UUID field + timestamps), with explicit on_delete on every ForeignKey, TextChoices enums in per-app constants.
pytest with pytest-django is the modern Django test stack.
Quality toolchain for Django projects: ruff (format + lint), mypy + django-stubs (typing), bandit (security), and pre-commit hooks orchestrating all of them.
Pattern for isolating Django business logic into a services/ layer of plain Python functions (or classes only when DI is required).
pytest-based testing patterns for Django projects using model_bakery (or factory_boy) for fixtures, @pytest.
Create AI-readable documentation pairs for every code directory: CLAUDE.
Convention for writing AI-readable directory context files: CLAUDE.

Domain-Driven Design (DDD) is a software development approach that models complex business domains through a shared ubiquitous language, explicit bounded contexts, and rich domain objects (entities, value objects, aggregates) that enforce business invariants.

Dual Write Outbox Decision Guide delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Monolith-to-services migration architecture (3 months)' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Outbox vs CDC vs dual-write is one of the highest-stakes decisions

Dual Write Shadow Read Template delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Big migration: Postgres major or monolith-to-services' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Migration playbooks need a reference implementation for dual-write +

Dx Friction Log Format delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Local dev env reset' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Capturing friction during env reset / setup feeds the DX backlog. No format / artifact for this exists in faion
Wave-based migration template anchoring tests, feature flags, codemods, and cutover for a typical 3-month framework / language migration (e.g., Cypress → Playwright, Vue 2 → Vue 3).

E2e Suite Parallelization delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Unflake and parallelize a slow E2E suite' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: E2E walltime is the most visible QA pain. Faion's e2e-testing covers POM + CI/CD basics
End-to-end testing with Playwright validates complete user journeys through the full application stack.
Guides writing maintainable E2E tests using the Page Object Model, handling authentication, API mocking, visual regression, and sharded CI pipelines.

E2e To Contract Migration Recipe delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Test pyramid rebalance: too-many-e2e to contract + unit' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Pyramid rebalance is high-value but has no walkthrough; current co

A methodology for standardizing API error responses following RFC 7807 Problem Details: every error returns `type` (URI), `title`, `status`, `detail`, `instance`, `traceId`, and an optional `errors[]` array for field-level validation failures.
Drive event-sourcing scaffolding as a five-stage agentic pipeline: aggregate design → code-gen → projection → tests → anti-pattern review.
The event-sourced aggregate root reconstructs its state by replaying a list of past events and emits new events (never mutating state directly) when commands succeed.
Event Sourcing persists the state of an aggregate as an ordered, immutable sequence of domain events rather than current state.
Event Sourcing persists the state of an entity as a sequence of state-changing events.

Implementation-level guide for event sourcing: event store (PostgreSQL), optimistic concurrency via stream versioning, snapshots to bound replay cost, projections that maintain read models, and an EventBus that routes stored events to projection handlers.
Projections listen to event streams and maintain specialized read models by applying events as idempotent UPSERTs keyed by (stream_id, position).
Snapshots periodically save the derived aggregate state so that reconstruction skips replaying the entire event history.
Once an event class is published (consumed by any downstream system), its field contract is immutable.

Evolutionary Architecture Fitness Functions delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'AI-drafted ADR with codebase-grounded review loop' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Beyond per-scenario fitness functions, architects need a meth
Session-based, charter-driven, heuristic-based exploratory testing (Bach/Bolton) — the discipline faion's scripted/automated testing corpus is missing.

Failure Mode As Design Input delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Microservice extraction safety gate' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: reliability-architecture covers SLOs and DR but not 'design the failure modes first'. A me

Retirement-side discipline for feature flags: every flag is born with an expiry, a removal owner, and a 'kill' definition so multi-month migrations and greenfield rollouts don't leave a sediment of stale toggles that quietly metastasize into tech debt.

Feature Flag Weekly Review Template delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Feature flag rollout decision (weekly)' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: feature-flags-* pages cover impl + targeting, but the operational weekly review
Feature flags gate incomplete or experimental code behind runtime toggles, decoupling deployment from release.

Figma Code Connect delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Design-system-as-code lifecycle: tokens → Storybook → Figma library → PR → governance' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: MCP Figma server exists with code-connect tooling,

Catalog index of all methodology folders in the `software-developer` skill, grouped by language and domain (Python, Django, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, Rust, Ruby, PHP, Java, C#, API Design, Testing, Frontend, Architecture, Dev Practices).

Fitness Function Suite Bootstrap delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Architecture fitness-function check (weekly)' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Fitness-functions are the operational form of architecture; quality-attribute

Map each high-priority QAA scenario to a concrete, scheduled CI job (k6, Gatling, Chaos Monkey, OWASP ZAP) with explicit breach actions, bridging architecture decisions and continuous verification instead of leaving fitness functions as a checkbox.

Flag Killswitch Decision Criteria delivers a concrete, testable methodology that turns the recurring task of 'Feature flag rollout decision (weekly)' into an auditable artefact, addressing the gap: Explicit criteria for kill vs keep vs ramp. Most teams default to 'leave it' which

Eliminate flaky tests by root-cause class (shared state, ordering, timing, network, locator fragility, infra) with one fix-pattern per class, a budgeted quarantine policy, and an explicit close-the-loop rule so the long tail of intermittents cannot rot.

Process playbook for flaky tests: detect via mechanical signal, quarantine within budget, bucket by responsible owner and severity, fix by class, close the loop with a 'held N runs' confirmation — the lifecycle existing testing-patterns refer to but do not specify.

Explicit decision rule for when a blocking upstream library bug should be patched via fork, upstream PR, monkey-patch, replacement, or workaround — with a 5-axis score (urgency / blast / patch size / upstream responsiveness / strategic dependency).
LLM-friendly code organization patterns for Django, Rails, Laravel, and React: service layer, selectors, DTOs, query objects, actions, and custom hooks.

Framework Major Upgrade Inventory — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. How to do the upfront forensics before any code touches: codemod inventory, breaking-change map, risk matrix. `framework-decomposition-patterns` exists but is about decomposition not version-jump.

A multi-phase workflow for solopreneur-scale UI projects: fix requirements (type, style, tech), generate 3-5 truly distinct design variants differing in navigation pattern and information density, render them to screenshots for comparison, select one based on human taste, define design tokens before components, scaffold Storybook with pinned versions, and deliver components colocated with stories and tests.
Create a new GitHub repository and configure it with production-grade defaults in one go: visibility, license,.
Production-grade Go backend pattern using the standard cmd/ + internal/ project layout with Gin or Echo routers.
Canonical Go goroutine and concurrency patterns: worker pool, rate limiter, semaphore, RWMutex cache, and sync.
Safe, leak-free concurrency in Go using bounded worker pools, fan-out/fan-in pipelines, and context.
Typed AppError with Code, Message, HTTPStatus, Err.
Go error handling convention for services: define sentinel errors in one apperror package; wrap with fmt.

A methodology for building Go HTTP services with Gin or Echo: explicit middleware stack (Recovery first, then Logger, then RequestID), thin handlers that only bind → call service → translate to HTTP, request structs with `binding:"required,.
Standard Go project layout following community conventions: cmd/ for entry points, internal/ for private app code, pkg/ only for truly public API.
Organizes a Go project into cmd/, internal/, pkg/, and migrations/ directories.

A methodology for building GraphQL APIs: schema-first SDL development with codegen, per-request DataLoader for every 1:N relation, depth limit (5-8) and query complexity cap before resolvers run, cursor-based (Relay) pagination for any list, and graphql-inspector diff in CI to block breaking schema changes.
Schema-first GraphQL design where the SDL is the source of truth, codegen drives client and server types, and every mutation returns a typed MutationNamePayload with a userErrors array.

Hidden Tech Debt Trace — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. `technical-debt` exists but is generic. Specific AI-era debt patterns (silent-skip tests, hallucinated abstractions, mock-only test coverage, copy-pasted Stack-Overflow-via-LLM) need their own catalogue + scan recipes.

Hipaa Phi Data Flow Design — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. HIPAA Security Rule + Privacy Rule impose concrete design constraints (minimum necessary, BAA boundary, audit controls §164.312(b)). Faion has zero HIPAA-specific methodology; the AI agent will silently log PHI or send it to embeddings APIs.

Hot Path Baseline Template — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. perf-test-* methodologies exist but no 'before-you-optimize, capture these N numbers' artifact. Prevents 'optimized for nothing' refactors.

Incident Decision Template — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. Architects making calls during incidents need a 2-min written-options template; without it decisions happen on voice channels and vanish.
Integration testing verifies that multiple components work correctly together using real dependencies (databases, APIs, message queues) or realistic test doubles.
Integration tests catch the class of bugs that unit tests cannot: ORM query bugs, constraint violations, serialization mismatches between layers, and authentication middleware failures.
Methodology for preparing software to support multiple languages and locales without code changes.

Ios Signing And Provisioning — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. iOS provisioning profiles, App Store Connect API keys, push certs, key rotation — none documented. P4 outsource devs ship to client store accounts and burn days every project on this.
JPA (Java Persistence API) with Hibernate provides a full-featured ORM for Spring Boot applications.
JUnit 5 (Jupiter) is the standard test framework for modern Spring Boot applications.
Spring Boot 3.
Spring's @Async + ThreadPoolTaskExecutor pattern for running methods on a managed thread pool without blocking the HTTP thread.
Structure Spring Boot applications with clean architecture.
Production-grade Spring Boot 3.
Universal coding standards for modern JS/TS (2025–2026): TypeScript 5.
A playbook for senior devs to spot AI-overreliance patterns during PR reviews and pairings, and to coach juniors back toward grounded engineering judgment.

Junior Next Pr Checklist Template — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. Concrete output of a mentoring review: a portable checklist the junior carries to their next PR. No methodology exists for this artifact.
A tier-0 decision router for stack selection.
Laravel 11 architecture patterns: thin controller → FormRequest validation → Action/Service → Eloquent model → API Resource.

Latency Budget Allocation — testable methodology for code, architecture, release, performance. performance-architecture exists but is generic. A 'latency budget' methodology splits end-to-end p95 across hops, marks each with an SLO, and produces a CI check. Pairs with reliability-architecture.
Maintenance health (last commit, open-issue trend, bus factor), license, bundle-size impact, security history, ecosystem fit — picking THIS library, not just build vs buy.

Architecture patterns that keep individual source files between 100-300 lines, use flat directory structures (max 3 levels), self-documenting file names, and explicit imports — designed to reduce LLM edit errors caused by large files and hidden import chains.

Architecture patterns that keep individual source files between 100-300 lines, use flat directory structures (max 3 levels), self-documenting file names, and explicit imports — designed to reduce LLM edit errors caused by large files and hidden import chains.

QA-facing pattern library for testing LLM hallucination — fact-checking probes, grounding-required questions, refusal-correctness tests, citation verification, contradiction tests, off-topic rejection.
Load Bearing Dep Criteria: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p6-product-dev-team/Security review for new dependency' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Load Profile Cookbook: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-qa-engineer/Performance + load test program rollout' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Lock In Risk Scoring Rubric: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Technology evaluation + selection cycle' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Emit structured JSON logs with correlation IDs, mask sensitive fields, use appropriate log levels, attach context via middleware, integrate with aggregators, prevent PII leaks.
Ls Gumroad Pricing Flip: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p2-indie-hacker/Pricing experiment toggle' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Asynchronous message passing between services using brokers (RabbitMQ, Redis Streams, Celery, SQS) with mandatory idempotent consumers, dead-letter queues (DLQ) with alerts, manual acknowledgment, and explicit retry policies.
A dispatcher index for the software-developer skill.
Microservice Extraction Decision Tree: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Microservice extraction safety gate' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
The circuit breaker wraps outbound calls to a dependency in a three-state machine: CLOSED (normal), OPEN (dependency down — fast-fail without calling it), and HALF_OPEN (recovery probe).

Microservices architecture structures an application as independently deployable services where each service owns its data, exposes a well-defined API, and communicates through HTTP or async messaging.
Microservices communicate either synchronously (HTTP/REST or gRPC for queries) or asynchronously (message bus for mutations and events).
Distributed tracing is the API for debugging microservices.
The saga pattern manages distributed transactions across microservices as a sequence of local transactions, each publishing an event or message triggering the next step.
Microservices architecture structures an application as a collection of loosely coupled, independently deployable services.
Migration Impact Mapping: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Major framework / language migration (3 months)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Whole-team real-time collaboration: 3–8 people sharing one screen, rotating driver every 5–15 minutes, all others navigating.

A team practice where all members work together on one task at one machine: a rotating Driver types only what the Navigator(s) dictate, and ideas must pass through someone else's hands before reaching the keyboard.
Mobile Device Matrix Strategy: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Ship a Mobile Build on iOS and Android' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Mobile Expo Eas Pipeline: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Ship a Mobile Build on iOS and Android' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Mobile-specific performance budget for iOS / Android — cold-start time, frame-drop ceiling, binary-size cap — with CI enforcement, distinct from web perf.
Mobile-responsive web design: viewport configuration, CSS media queries, flexible layouts, touch-friendly interactions, and mobile-first development for devices from 320px to 2560px.
Test doubles (stubs, mocks, spies, fakes) isolate code under test from external dependencies.
Covers the five test-double types (Dummy/Stub/Spy/Mock/Fake), decision framework for what to mock (mock at boundaries, not internals), Python unittest.
Turborepo is a high-performance build system for JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos.
Multi Region Failover Pattern Pack: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Disaster-recovery plan + live drill' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Mutation Testing Bootstrap: codified testing practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Test-coverage rebuild from 30% to 70% (6 weeks)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Adopt mutation testing (Stryker / Mutmut / Pitest) as a CI quality gate so assertion-laxness becomes visible — especially for AI-generated tests that hit line coverage without actually testing behavior.
New Dependency Risk Checklist: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p6-product-dev-team/Security review for new dependency' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Modern Next.
A methodology for building production Node.
Controller-Service-Repository pattern for Node.
Data modeling methodology for document (MongoDB), key-value (Redis), wide-column (Cassandra), and graph (Neo4j) stores.
Observability As Design Input: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-architect/Architecture review meeting facilitation' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Observability In Code: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Make Production Readiness a PR-Level Concern' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
One Command Dev Env Template: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Local dev env reset' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
OpenAPI 3.

Outsource Pr Etiquette: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Feature from spec to production (3 weeks, P4 client-rules edition)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

A maintainable page-object pattern for Playwright and Cypress at scale, with a locator-convention discipline (role > test-id > css), one-method-per-user-action API, no implicit waits, and a per-page contract test that prevents selector drift from rotting the suite.
A practice where two developers share a single workstation — one drives, one navigates.
A practice where two programmers share one workstation: the Driver writes code while the Navigator reviews each line, thinks strategically, and catches errors.

Pair With Ai Agent Protocol: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Pair / mob session with junior on AI-assisted task' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.

Pci Dss Scope Minimisation: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'p4-outsource-specialist/Compliance-Grade Feature Delivery (FinTech / HIPAA / PCI)' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Perf Budget As Code: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Make Production Readiness a PR-Level Concern' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Perf Budget Pr Policy: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-qa-engineer/Performance + load test program rollout' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Perf Regression Alert Recipe: codified dev practice that turns the recurring 'role-software-developer/Performance profiling pass on one hot path' decision into a repeatable, auditable artefact.
Performance budgets (Core Web Vitals, p95 latency, payload size, cold-start) wired as CI gates with reasoned thresholds — what QA engineers own in P6 product teams.
A discipline for validating that applications meet latency, throughput, and stability targets under realistic load.

Eloquent ORM patterns for Laravel 10/11: model with $fillable, enum casts, scopes, accessors/mutators; optional thin Repository or Query Object for multi-condition filters; eager loading via with()/loadMissing(); Model::preventLazyLoading() in development; pagination enforced on all user-facing endpoints.
Production Laravel backend patterns: Controller → FormRequest → Service → Eloquent model → API Resource slice.

Layered Laravel architecture: thin HTTP controllers, service classes that own business logic and transactions, Eloquent models accessed through service methods, and API Resources for response shaping.

Laravel's queue system for offloading background work: job classes implementing ShouldQueue, named queues for priority, WithoutOverlapping middleware for deduplication, retry/backoff/timeout configuration, and Bus::batch for parallel dataset processing.

Laravel PHPUnit test patterns: feature tests via Tests\TestCase + RefreshDatabase covering happy path, validation failures, auth/authorization failures, and 404s; unit tests mocking dependencies; and Http::fake() / Queue::fake() / Mail::fake() for all outgoing side effects.
Covers pnpm configuration, workspace management, CI/CD integration, and security practices for JavaScript/TypeScript projects.

Post Migration Cleanup — pinned method for the software developer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so survive a major framework upgrade without a big-bang branch stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

PR Mentoring Session Protocol — pinned protocol for the product-dev team: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so pr review with junior present (mentoring loop) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

PR Pattern Mining For Feedback — pinned method for the software architect: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so junior / mid dev architecture mentoring (weekly 1:1) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Prompt Patterns For Common DEV Tasks — pinned method for the software developer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so merge ai-generated code without bleeding stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
Methodology for turning a web app into an installable Progressive Web App with offline capability via Workbox service workers, a compliant Web App Manifest, and a user-driven install/update prompt.

Patterns for the full Python development stack: Poetry for dependency management, Django models/views/services/admin, FastAPI routes/dependencies/schemas, pytest fixtures/mocking/parametrize, type hints with `mypy --strict`, ruff formatting, venv/pyenv, and asyncio concurrency patterns.
Async/await patterns for Python 3.
Production-grade FastAPI: Pydantic v2 schemas define the API contract, `Depends()` injects DB sessions and auth, async routes call service functions, lifespan events manage connection pools.
Poetry is the standard for Python dependency management: deterministic builds via poetry.
Modern Python typing (3.

A mapping discipline that ties every acceptance criterion in a user story to a named, behavior-asserting test, so the PR review can verify each AC has a real assertion (not just a structural test) before merge.

QA Bug Bash Runbook — pinned runbook for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so weekly bug-bash facilitation (friday) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
A diff-only coverage dashboard wired into PRs so the visible metric is changed-lines coverage (not whole-repo coverage) — the only honest signal of test investment per change.

QA Edge Case Spec Template — pinned template for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so test data setup for tricky scenario stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

A session-based exploratory testing charter template (Bach/Bolton style) that turns vague "have a poke around" into a structured 60-90 minute session with mission, focus areas, time-boxed test ideas, observations, and a debrief — the single highest-yield human technique against AI-generated code.

QA Flake Ledger Template — pinned template for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so daily flaky-test triage (morning queue) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Flaky Test Root Cause Taxonomy — pinned taxonomy for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so daily flaky-test triage (morning queue) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Metrics Slos — pinned method for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so modernize a 2018-era qa suite into ai-augmented test ops stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Perf Run Verdict Template — pinned template for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so performance test run + analysis stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Prioritization Rubric — pinned rubric for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so modernize a 2018-era qa suite into ai-augmented test ops stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA RC Smoke Pack Template — pinned template for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so release-candidate smoke pack stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

A maintained QA risk matrix that scores every product area on impact × likelihood, drives test-investment allocation (unit / integration / e2e / exploratory), and updates per release so testing strategy stays in sync with what is actually risky.

QA Rollback Trigger Canon — pinned canon for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so release-candidate smoke pack stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Security Triage Runbook — pinned runbook for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so security scan triage (weekly) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Session Based Test Management — pinned method for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so exploratory testing session on new feature stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Suite Health Metrics Canon — pinned canon for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so test-suite health dashboard review stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

QA Test Data Catalog — pinned method for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so test data setup for tricky scenario stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

A decision framework for choosing test pyramid vs Kent C. Dodds testing trophy per module — based on stack, change frequency, integration risk, and runtime cost — so the team stops the "too many e2e and too slow CI" failure mode without overcorrecting.

QA Test Strategy Template — pinned template for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so test strategy from scratch for a new product stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

API protection via configurable per-key rate limiters (fixed window, sliding window, token bucket) backed by Redis/Valkey, with standard X-RateLimit-* / Retry-After response headers and a 429 JSON error body.
A directory layout and component design system for React/Next.

A methodology for writing correct React functional components using hooks: call hooks unconditionally at the top level, list exhaustive dependencies, return cleanup from every effect, and prefer derivation over synced state.

Readiness Matrix Template — pinned template for the software architect: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so pre-incident readiness review (weekly 30-min) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Refactor Baseline Metrics Script — pinned method for the software developer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so two-hour refactor block on one module stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

A closed catalog of eight structure-preserving transforms — extract method/class, replace conditional with polymorphism, introduce parameter object, replace magic numbers, decompose conditional, rename for clarity, move method — applied one at a time behind a green test suite.

Catalog of structural code transformations that improve readability and maintainability without changing external behavior: Extract Method/Function, Extract Class, Replace Conditional with Polymorphism, Introduce Parameter Object, Replace Magic Numbers, Decompose Conditional, Rename for Clarity, Move Method.

Regression Test First Bugfix Workflow — pinned method for the software developer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so sentry/datadog alert → bugfix → ship stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Release QA Cycle Template — pinned template for the QA engineer: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so major release qa cycle: regression + smoke + uat stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.
Resource-oriented HTTP API design conventions: plural noun paths, correct HTTP method semantics, standard status codes, and consistent filter/pagination parameters.
Sanctioned no-blame workflow for capturing architectural decisions AFTER code has already shipped — Retroactive ADRs — so undocumented decisions become audit-able without freezing delivery.

RFC Template Product DEV Team — pinned template for the product-dev team: fixed shape + named owner + evidence anchors + outcome review, so rfc → production: feature delivery (3-6 weeks) stops being folklore and starts being a reviewable operating tool.

Efficient database access with proper query optimization via Query Objects for chainable filters, scopes as lambdas for composition, eager loading via includes() to prevent N+1, and lifecycle callbacks (before_validation, after_create_commit).
Rails backend patterns for production-grade applications.

Rails service object pattern: extract multi-step business workflows from fat controllers into app/services/BoundedContext/ActionService classes with a single call method that returns a ServiceResult (success/failure).
Write comprehensive, maintainable tests for Rails applications using RSpec 3.

Sidekiq job patterns for Rails apps: job class with `include Sidekiq::Job`, explicit `sidekiq_options` (queue, retry, dead), idempotency check at the top of `perform`, primitive IDs in arguments (never ActiveRecord objects), custom retry backoff via `sidekiq_retry_in`, and RSpec tests using `Sidekiq::Testing.
Result<T, E> for fallible operations, Option<T> for optional values, ? operator for ergonomic propagation.
Ownership eliminates memory errors at compile time.

Testing patterns for Rust services: unit tests with `mockall` in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` co-located with the SUT, async tests via `#[tokio::test]`, integration tests against real `axum::Router` via `tower::ServiceExt`, and property tests with `proptest`.
Tokio multiplexes thousands of I/O-bound connections on a small thread pool.
Pre-authored bundle of canonical security ADR stubs (auth, encryption, secrets, isolation, residency) that accelerates security-by-design audits.
STRIDE-lite per-feature threat-model checklist that runs before a feature design is merged, producing a small artefact the architecture review consumes.
Security testing identifies vulnerabilities before exploitation through static analysis (SAST), dynamic testing (DAST), dependency scanning, and OWASP Top 10 scenario tests.

Layered security testing using SAST (static code analysis), DAST (dynamic attack simulation), SCA (dependency vulnerability scanning), and secret detection — integrated at every stage of the CI/CD pipeline.
A methodology for making JavaScript-heavy applications indexable.
Day-0 greenfield service bootstrap checklist consolidating layout, CI, AGENTS.md, container, observability stubs, secrets, and release into a single ship-ready artefact.
shadcn/ui is a component system where primitives are copied into `components/ui/` (not installed as a package), styled with Tailwind CSS, managed with CVA variants, and built on Radix Primitives.
Copy-into-codebase component architecture using shadcn/ui: Radix UI primitives + Tailwind + CVA variants.

Weekly 30-minute architecture ritual that converts the past week's SLO burn data into named ADR-worthy decisions, paired with the burn-decision matrix, so error budgets become a live architectural input rather than wallpaper.
Self-code-review protocol for a solo founder reviewing their own (or AI-generated) PRs — checklist, AI as second reviewer, 24h cool-off for risky changes.
Failing spec first, then minimize repro, then fix — bug-scale SDD that the feature-scale SDD doesn't cover.

Profile queries with `EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)` before touching any index or query, add composite indexes with equality columns first and range columns last, replace OFFSET pagination with keyset cursors, and batch inserts/updates.

Trade-off framing methodology for an outsource senior dev writing an ADR inside a client-mandated stack — names the constraint as policy, frames options within the stack, and uses costed mitigations instead of "we recommend a different stack" language.
Daily-use staging smoke checklist organised by surface ("top-3 surfaces, what to click, what to verify"), produced once per surface and re-run before every promotion to production.

Codifies Storybook as the canonical Source of Truth for a design system — story-per-state discipline, a11y addon enforcement, token-first composition, and the tokens → Storybook → Figma library → PR governance loop.
Storybook v8/v9 setup for React component libraries: `main.
Named, stepped-out incremental-migration pattern for framework upgrades (Vue 2→3, Next pages→app router, Rails major, Django major) that avoids the big-bang branch.

Team-execution checklist for big migrations (Postgres major, monolith-to-services, region migration) — legacy contract freeze, ACL rollout, traffic-shift gates, sunset criteria — distinct from the pattern essay.
Stride Lite Checklist For Architects: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-architect/Security review of a new dependency or service edge'.
Stride Threat Model Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-architect/Security-by-design audit + threat-modelling cycle'.
Stripe Payment Links No Backend: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p2-indie-hacker/Distribution-First Idea Validation (audience before product)'.
Canonical payment-webhook recipe: signature verification, idempotency key, replay handling, dead-letter.
Stripe Webhook Hardening: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p1-solo-saas-builder/Pre-launch hardening: vibe-coded MVP → safe-to-bill production'.
Structured Logging As Code: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-developer/Make Production Readiness a PR-Level Concern'.
Supabase-specific patterns: Auth + RLS + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions wired as a coherent MVP stack.

A 7-signal checklist for evaluating a library before adoption (license, maintainer count, last release, CVE history, GitHub star vs npm-download ratio, transitive depth, vendor lock-in) producing a numeric risk score.
Utility-first CSS framework where styles are composed from atomic classes directly in markup.
A utility-first CSS architecture for React/Vue/Svelte apps: design tokens live in `tailwind.
Take Home Rubric Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p6-product-dev-team/Hiring screen / take-home review'.
Red-Green-Refactor cycle for test-driven development: write one failing test, write minimal code to pass it, then improve structure without changing behavior.

Covers the Red-Green-Refactor loop adapted for LLM-assisted development, why tests-as-spec reduce total tokens, PostToolUse hooks that enforce the RED step, per-behavior TDD cycle scripting, and the conditions under which TDD costs more than it saves (exploratory spikes, UI prototypes).
Team Conventions As Code: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-developer/Make Production Readiness a PR-Level Concern'.
Dev-scope RFC template + lifecycle (draft → review → accepted/rejected → archived) for P6 product-team devs.
A framework for making technical debt visible, typed, and actionable.
Tech Debt Budget For Solos: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p1-solo-saas-builder/AI-pair coding loop for solo SaaS (Claude/Cursor + Spec)'.
Tech Debt Cost Of Delay Scoring: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-architect/Weekly tech-debt prioritisation session'.

Prioritization and payoff strategy for technical debt using a scoring formula (Impact x Risk x Interest / Cost), four execution strategies (Boy Scout, feature-attached, dedicated sprint, Strangler Fig), and CI/pre-commit gates to prevent accumulation.
Tech Debt Sprint Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-developer/Quarterly tech-debt sprint (2 weeks)'.
Technology Evaluation Rubric: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-architect/Cost + latency budget BEFORE design freeze'.
BDD-style readable tests, doc-string-driven test generation, Given-When-Then enforcement — testing for documentation fidelity, not just regression.

Patterns for creating reusable, isolated test data: pytest fixtures with yield-based cleanup, factory functions with keyword-only overrides, database session rollback fixtures, and TypeScript factory helpers.

Covers fixture creation patterns (Factory Boy, Builder, Object Mother), pytest fixture scopes and composition, database isolation strategies (transactional rollback, Django test DB, SQLAlchemy), cleanup with yield, and conftest organization.
Test Pyramid Policy Enforcement: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-qa-engineer/Test pyramid rebalance: too-many-e2e to contract + unit'.
Test Pyramid Rebalance Playbook: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-qa-engineer/Modernize a 2018-era QA suite into AI-augmented test ops'.
Test Suite Audit Rubric: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-qa-engineer/Modernize a 2018-era QA suite into AI-augmented test ops'.
Comprehensive testing patterns for pytest (Python), Jest/Vitest (JavaScript/TypeScript), Go's testing package, Playwright (E2E), and Cypress.
Covers Go's standard `testing` package patterns: table-driven tests with `t.

Covers the Vitest vs Jest decision tree (2025), unit and component testing patterns, React Testing Library integration, MSW v2 for API mocking, fake timers, coverage configuration, and common pitfalls (ESM/CJS confusion, jsdom layout limitations, MSW v1→v2 migration, fake timer leaks).
Proven structural and data patterns for writing maintainable, reliable tests — applicable across Python, TypeScript, and Go.
Covers pytest configuration, fixture scopes and composition, parametrize patterns, mocking with unittest.
Threat Model As Code: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p4-outsource-specialist/Compliance-Grade Feature Delivery (FinTech / HIPAA / PCI)'.
Threat Model Lite Workshop: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-qa-engineer/Security testing program rollout'.
Timeboxed Refactor Session Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-developer/Two-hour refactor block on one module'.
Trust Boundary Diff Helper: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-architect/Security review of a new dependency or service edge'.

TypeScript strict mode is a compiler flag group (`strict: true` + four recommended extras) that eliminates implicit `any`, enforces null safety, catches unchecked index access, and requires explicit return types.
Uat Script Pattern: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-qa-engineer/Major release QA cycle: regression + smoke + UAT'.

Versioned, semver-controlled React component library with a layered structure (primitives → composite → patterns → layout), consistent prop API doctrine, design token integration, co-located Storybook stories + tests, and Radix UI/React Aria for interactive primitives.
A methodology for writing isolated, deterministic tests that verify a single behavior per test using the Arrange-Act-Assert pattern.

Covers the FIRST properties (Fast/Isolated/Repeatable/Self-validating/Timely), Arrange-Act-Assert structure, test naming conventions (method-scenario-expected, should-when, given-when-then), coverage strategies (line vs branch vs mutation), test categories, and the most damaging anti-patterns (testing implementation, not behavior).
V1 To V2 Migration Playbook: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p1-solo-saas-builder/Pivot from failed v1 to v2'.
Vendor Eval Pilot Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-architect/Technology evaluation + selection cycle'.
Vendor Exit Cost Estimator: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-architect/Vendor / library evaluation gate'.
Visual Regression Baselining: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-qa-engineer/Unflake and parallelize a slow E2E suite'.
Visual Regression Testing: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-ux-ui-designer/Design-system-as-code lifecycle: tokens → Storybook → Figma library → PR → governance'.
Vuln Triage Sla Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-qa-engineer/Security testing program rollout'.
Triage rubric for accessibility audit findings into blocker / serious / moderate / minor with assistive-tech, WCAG level, and user-journey criteria.
WebSocket provides full-duplex, persistent connections between clients and servers.
Weekly Arch Review Agenda Template: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'p6-product-dev-team/Weekly architectural review (45 min)'.
Weekly Branch Hygiene Checklist: produces a versioned, owner-signed artefact that closes the gap 'role-software-developer/Pre-merge friday cleanup (rebase + squash + changelog)'.
Agile methodology built on 5 values (communication, simplicity, feedback, courage, respect) and 12 practices (TDD, pair programming, collective ownership, CI, small releases, YAGNI).

An agile methodology built on 12 engineering practices taken to their logical extreme: TDD (test everything), continuous integration (integrate many times daily), pair programming (review all code), simple design (fewest elements that pass), and collective ownership (anyone changes anything).
